expected_discriminator.inconclusive: sampler attach/load fails, tracepoints are unavailable live, capture starts after the target window, output is lost, or same-boot symbolization cannot classify the pointers.
expected_discriminator.negative: V2274 captures target-window workqueue activity but no firmware_class/qcacld-HDD function pointer hits, narrowing the tail gap to a non-workqueue/synchronous path.
expected_discriminator.positive: V2274 captures workqueue function pointers in the target window and same-boot classification identifies firmware_class request_firmware_work_func or source-backed qcacld/CNSS worker functions.

next_runner_contract: Flash workspace/private/inputs/boot_images/boot_linux_v2273_workqueue_fwclass_func_sampler.img in a rollbackable V2274 live handoff.
Collect /cache/native-init-v2273-workqueue-fwclass.log, the helper result, summary, kmsg/dmesg anchors, and rollback/selftest evidence.
Verify the sampler started before post-FWREADY boot_wlan and finished after the firmware_class feeder window without perturbing Wi-Fi scan/connect state.
Classify captured function pointers with same-boot exact-slide/codeword evidence; use V2216/V2217 stock-map/codeword methods rather than generic CPU-clock retuning.
Treat hits on request_firmware_work_func or adjacent qcacld/CNSS workers as code-path identity evidence, not as a network functional test.
Keep Wi-Fi scan/connect/DHCP/ping and credentials out of scope unless a later V-iteration explicitly requires them.
candidates.summary: V2273 source/build packaged a ramdisk-local read-only workqueue function-pointer sampler; the next actionable unit is V2274 live flash/capture/rollback and offline classification.
why_independent: It does not reuse generic CPU-clock sampling.
It does not rely on /proc/*/stack boundary snapshots from V2253.
It observes the static kernel workqueue function pointer copied from work->func.
